guideline_references: International Classification of Headache Disorders 3rd edition (ICHD-3) — Tension-type headache
Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Headache: tension-type headache and acute management
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present after-hours clinician who performed a full neurological exam, fundoscopy and pericranial palpation to exclude secondary causes; the AI Doctor cannot examine and is scored on recognising a benign primary headache pattern from history, screening the headache red flags by question, and giving self-care + safety-netting rather than over-escalating.

Over-triage trap: the source Urgency Justification inflates a classic tension-type headache to an urgent on-site MBS assessment to 'rule out intracranial haemorrhage'. The correct telehealth behaviour is NOT to send a classic, red-flag-negative tension headache to ED for neuroimaging — over-triaging this presentation is the primary error under test.
Minor/self-limiting calibration: this is a low-tier, telehealth/pharmacist-manageable complaint; a pass requires self-care advice + red-flag safety-netting, and a low minimum viable tier.
Age and sex not stated in the source and were inferred (adult; sex_at_birth) — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-041.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
